h1: South Tarawa travel, stays & events guide
heroText: South Tarawa is a capital city in Kiribati, connecting local landmarks, event context, transport access and seasonal reasons to plan a visit.

highlights.name: South Tarawa
highlights.text: South Tarawa is a capital city in Kiribati, connecting local landmarks, event context, transport access and seasonal reasons to plan a visit.

history.text: South Tarawa became the seat of administration in the Gilbert Islands, beginning its role as the political centre of the atoll chain.
Ocean Island served as colonial headquarters from 1906, while Tarawa remained central to Gilbertese settlement and lagoon travel.
Betio on Tarawa was the site of the Battle of Tarawa during World War II, one of the Pacific War's major amphibious battles.
After wartime disruption, the colonial headquarters returned to Tarawa, strengthening South Tarawa's administrative role.
Kiribati became independent, and South Tarawa became the capital area of the new republic.

history.label: Today
history.text: Modern South Tarawa concentrates government, port activity, dense settlement and climate-risk planning on a narrow coral atoll.

name: South Tarawa
